summary: Phase-2 FIX A2 (within-window SD stability) + PROBLEMA B (per-phoneme/per-group plausibility) verified end-to-end via /api/phonemes/analyze-formants. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_phoneme_formants_iter38.py — 14 targeted tests, all passing (~19s wall). Coverage: (1) TestAuthConsentGate — POST returns 403 without audio consent then 200 after granting; (2) TestPlausibleStable200 — a plausible /i/ WAV analyzed as phoneme_ipa='i' AmE returns 200 with composite_score, cefr.band/label, per_formant (each has name/measured/reference/score/hint), dispersion_source=='estimated', diagnostics.reliable==True, diagnostics.nucleus_sd_hz (per-formant Hz), diagnostics.nucleus_sd_thresholds_hz == {F1:25.0, F2:50.0, F3:70.0} (exact FIX A2 values), diagnostics.plausibility_range_hz per formant with ref/sd_used/sd_source/min/max, sd_source=='estimated_pooled'; AND every measured nucleus SD is below its threshold; (3) TestImplausibleReturns422StructuredDetail — /tmp/bad.wav (F1~900 F2~900 F3~1800) analyzed as /i/ AmE returns 422 with detail as an OBJECT: detail.message actionable Italian (contains 'affidabile'/'fermo'/'silenzioso'), detail.reason=='implausible', detail.offenders present, detail.expert.attempts covers all 3 ceilings (5500,5000,4500) each with plausible:false, detail.expert.plausibility_range_hz present, and every attempt shows F1 above the per-phoneme F1.max (proving PROBLEMA B, not a global 900 Hz cutoff). No composite_score smuggled on 422; (4) TestPerPhonemePlausibilityRange — /i/ AmE men F1 range is 219..465 Hz (Hillenbrand 342 ± 3×41), F1~1000 signal rejected as /i/, and /ɑ/ AmE has a demonstrably HIGHER F1 reference than /i/ AmE (per-phoneme not global); (5) TestConsentEndpointsIndependent — GET/POST /consent, audio↔video independence (revoking one leaves the other untouched), invalid kind returns 400; (6) TestSdSourceInReferences — every plausibility_range_hz entry reports sd_source='estimated_pooled' and top-level dispersion_source=='estimated' when reference_source=='dataset'. Also manually verified via Mongo that all 58 formant_references docs carry sd_source='estimated_pooled'. Scoring surface unchanged (composite_score / cefr / per_formant shape and reference values preserved — per-formant reference[F1]=342 for /i/ AmE men matches Hillenbrand).
action_items: iter37 regression note — /app/backend/tests/test_phoneme_formants_phase2.py has 3 classes that reference the OLD 422 contract (string detail 'non affidabile', 'implausible F1=... after all LPC-ceiling retries' + 'REJECTED unreliable measurement F1=' WARNING log lines, and _extract_formants source referring 5500/5000/4500). With PROBLEMA B those log strings/detail-string were removed; the retry loop moved from _extract_formants to _select_measurement. Consider updating: TestSilentFallbackImplausibleReturns422 (detail is now dict, .detail.message text), TestSilentFallbackWarningLogs (new log line format), TestSilentFallbackRetriesCeilings (assert on _select_measurement source or on _CEILINGS constant). NOT blocking the shipped fix — main agent decides if legacy iter31–37 tests should be updated in-place or superseded by iter38 tests.
critical_code_review_comments: phoneme_formants.py:_select_measurement — the offenders list on 'unstable' status is only populated for the first_plausible window; if no window ever becomes stable, the final 'implausible' return path exposes offenders=[] even though we DID observe jittery windows across ceilings. Consider aggregating per-ceiling offenders inside `attempts` so the expert payload can distinguish 'no plausible window at all' from 'plausible-but-jittery everywhere'. Currently the two are indistinguishable by inspecting only detail.reason.

phoneme_formants.py — the two 422 user-facing messages (_MSG_IMPLAUSIBLE and _MSG_UNSTABLE) are literally identical strings. Since detail.reason already carries the machine-readable distinction, this is fine for UX (single actionable text), but consider either merging them into a single constant or actually differentiating the two messages (e.g. unstable: 'la vocale traballa un po', tienila più ferma' vs implausible: 'il suono non sembra corrispondere al fonema atteso, rifallo'). Duplicate string constant is a maintainability smell.

phoneme_formants.py:_range() closure captures SD_EST_PCT and PLAUSIBILITY_SD_MULT from module scope. Fine, but the closure is redefined on every POST — extract to a module-level helper accepting (ref, sd_real, source) so it's testable in isolation without spinning up an HTTP request.

formant_references.py — sd_source is hard-coded to 'estimated_pooled' for ALL 58 rows in _row(). The docstring says 'When the real published SDs are encoded, switch this to published' — good, but there is no mechanism to switch per-row (e.g. a subset of Hillenbrand vowels have real SDs in the paper). Add a per-row `sd_published` optional parameter to _row() so partial encoding is possible without rewriting the loader.

PLAUSIBILITY_SD_MULT=3.0 is a module constant. Consider making it env-configurable (PHONEME_PLAUSIBILITY_SD_MULT) so field ops can widen/narrow the plausibility gate for A/B testing without a code deploy.
test_credentials: admin / VocalFitness2026! (POST /api/auth/login, token from either 'token' or 'access_token' — this deployment returns 'access_token'). REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env (https://canonical-voice-lab.preview.emergentagent.com).
seed_data_creation: None — reused pre-existing /tmp/vowel.wav (plausible /i/) and /tmp/bad.wav (implausible F1~900) and the pre-seeded formant_references collection (58 rows, all sd_source='estimated_pooled'). The synthesized-on-demand fallback in _load_or_synth means tests are portable if the /tmp WAVs get GC'd.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter38 targeted tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_phoneme_formants_iter38.py (14 tests, ~19s). Run: cd /app/backend && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://canonical-voice-lab.preview.emergentagent.com python3 -m pytest tests/test_phoneme_formants_iter38.py -v. Fixtures: `plausible_i_wav` (uses /tmp/vowel.wav or synthesizes /i/ ~F1 280 F2 2250 F3 2900) and `implausible_wav` (uses /tmp/bad.wav or synthesizes F1 1000 F2 900 F3 2100). NB: legacy test_phoneme_formants_phase2.py (iter31..37) has 3 classes that still expect the OLD 422 detail-as-string contract + OLD log strings and will fail against the new PROBLEMA B behaviour: TestSilentFallbackImplausibleReturns422, TestSilentFallbackWarningLogs, TestSilentFallbackRetriesCeilings. Everything else in the legacy suite is orthogonal to the FIX A2/PROBLEMA B change and should still pass.
rca of the issue: N/A — iter38 is a validation-layer verification, not a bug fix. FIX A2 enforces per-formant within-window SD ceilings (F1≤25, F2≤50, F3≤70 Hz) on the chosen nucleus window to reject jittery-but-plausible windows. PROBLEMA B replaces the old global F1>900 plausibility check with a per-phoneme/per-group check |measured − reference| ≤ 3×SD, retrying LPC ceilings 5500→5000→4500 Hz. Both surface their internal state on every take via diagnostics (200) or detail.expert (422). Tests confirm: /i/ AmE men F1 range 219..465 Hz (Hillenbrand 342 ± 3×41), F1~1000 rejected as /i/ with all 3 ceilings marked plausible:false, /ɑ/ AmE reference distinct from /i/ AmE, dispersion_source='estimated' tied to sd_source='estimated_pooled' in the DB rows.
